description: Bookings Backend — PR #13 rework: fix empty migration, extract shared mapper, document skipped tests

userStories.title: Regenerate AddBookings migration with proper DDL
userStories.description: The 20260306141947_AddBookings migration has empty Up() and Down() methods — the Bookings and BookingServices tables will never be created. The ModelSnapshot was updated, so EF Core thinks the tables exist and a subsequent migration will not include them either. This must be fixed.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `dotnet ef migrations remove --project src/backend/Chairly.Infrastructure --startup-project src/backend/Chairly.Api` to remove the broken AddBookings migration and revert the snapshot
Run `dotnet ef migrations add AddBookings --project src/backend/Chairly.Infrastructure --startup-project src/backend/Chairly.Api` to regenerate the migration

Verify the regenerated Up() method contains migrationBuilder.CreateTable for 'Bookings' with all columns (Id, TenantId, ClientId, StaffMemberId, StartTime, EndTime, Notes, CreatedAtUtc, CreatedBy, UpdatedAtUtc, UpdatedBy, ConfirmedAtUtc, ConfirmedBy, StartedAtUtc, StartedBy, CompletedAtUtc, CompletedBy, CancelledAtUtc, CancelledBy, NoShowAtUtc, NoShowBy)

Verify the regenerated Up() method contains migrationBuilder.CreateTable for 'BookingServices' with columns (Id, BookingId, ServiceId, ServiceName, Duration, Price, SortOrder) and a FK constraint to Bookings
Verify the regenerated Up() method contains migrationBuilder.CreateIndex calls for IX_Bookings_TenantId, IX_Bookings_TenantId_StaffMemberId, and IX_Bookings_TenantId_StartTime
Verify the regenerated Down() method contains DropTable calls for both tables
Run dotnet format on the generated migration files: `dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx`
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: The `dotnet ef migrations remove` command does NOT require a running PostgreSQL — EF Core only needs to build the project and read the snapshot file. Similarly, `dotnet ef migrations add` compares the model against the snapshot without connecting to a database. The DesignTimeDbContextFactory (UseNpgsql) just configures the provider for SQL generation, not an actual connection. After regeneration, check the Up() method is NOT empty. If it is still empty (should not happen), read the AddClient migration at `src/backend/Chairly.Infrastructure/Migrations/20260306071435_AddClient.cs` for the exact pattern and manually write the CreateTable statements based on the BookingConfiguration and the Designer.cs model.

userStories.title: Extract ToResponse and DeriveStatus to shared BookingMapper
userStories.description: The ToResponse() and DeriveStatus() methods currently live inside GetBookingsListHandler but are referenced by CreateBookingHandler, UpdateBookingHandler, and GetBookingHandler. This creates cross-slice coupling. Extract them to a shared class within the Bookings context.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Chairly.Api/Features/Bookings/BookingMapper.cs: internal static class BookingMapper with `internal static BookingResponse ToResponse(Booking b)` and `private static string DeriveStatus(Booking b)` — move the exact logic from GetBookingsListHandler.ToResponse and GetBookingsListHandler.DeriveStatus
Update GetBookingsListHandler.cs: remove the ToResponse and DeriveStatus methods, use BookingMapper.ToResponse instead
Update GetBookingHandler.cs: replace GetBookingsListHandler.ToResponse(booking) with BookingMapper.ToResponse(booking), update using statements
Update CreateBookingHandler.cs: replace GetBookingsListHandler.ToResponse(booking) with BookingMapper.ToResponse(booking), remove the `using Chairly.Api.Features.Bookings.GetBookingsList;` import
Update UpdateBookingHandler.cs: replace GetBookingsListHandler.ToResponse(booking) with BookingMapper.ToResponse(booking), remove the `using Chairly.Api.Features.Bookings.GetBookingsList;` import
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: The BookingMapper goes in the Bookings root folder (Chairly.Api/Features/Bookings/BookingMapper.cs) alongside BookingResponse.cs and BookingServiceResponse.cs. It should be an internal static class. DeriveStatus should be private since only ToResponse uses it. The status derivation priority order must be preserved: Cancelled → NoShow → Completed → InProgress → Confirmed → Scheduled. Do NOT change the ToResponse logic — only move it.

userStories.title: Add TODO comments on skipped UpdateBooking tests
userStories.description: Two UpdateBookingHandler tests are skipped due to InMemory provider OwnsMany limitations. Add TODO comments documenting that these need integration test coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: On the Skip attribute of UpdateBookingHandler_HappyPath_UpdatesBookingAndRecalculatesEndTime, update the Skip message to include: 'InMemory provider cannot handle OwnsMany + Add + SaveChanges — needs PostgreSQL integration test. TODO: Cover with Testcontainers or Aspire-based integration test.'

On the Skip attribute of UpdateBookingHandler_OverlapExcludesSelf_NoConflict, update the Skip message to include: 'InMemory provider cannot handle OwnsMany + Add + SaveChanges — needs PostgreSQL integration test. TODO: Cover with Testcontainers or Aspire-based integration test.'
dotnet build src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet test src/backend/Chairly.slnx passes
dotnet format src/backend/Chairly.slnx --verify-no-changes passes
userStories.notes: Only change the Skip message string. Do not modify any test logic.
